perturbation.original_text: Each Party also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Neither Party is under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction, and either Party may terminate discussions and negotiations with the other Party at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement. However, both parties will engage in good-faith negotiations. Neither Party is under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction, and either Party may terminate discussions and negotiations with the other Party at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that no legal obligation exists regarding the 'Possible Transaction' until a final agreement is signed, except for matters specifically agreed upon in the current agreement (NDA). Inserting 'However, both parties will engage in good-faith negotiations' creates a contradictory legal obligation to negotiate in good faith, even though the previous sentence says there is no obligation regarding the possible transaction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Not a Transaction Agreement . Each Party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered by both Parties. Each Party also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement. However, both parties will engage in good-faith negotiations. Neither Party is under any obligation to accept any proposal regarding a Possible Transaction, and either Party may terminate discussions and negotiations with the other Party at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that neither party is under any legal obligation regarding a 'Possible Transaction' until a final agreement is signed, except for matters specifically agreed upon in the current agreement. The changed text introduces 'However, both parties will engage in good-faith negotiations' which imposes a legal obligation to negotiate in good faith. This directly contradicts the prior statement that there is no legal obligation regarding the possible transaction, as stated in the 'Contradicted Text'. Therefore, it's a strong in-text contradiction as it imposes an obligation where one explicitly was disclaimed.

perturbation.original_text: No provision of this Agreement can be waived or amended in favor of either Party except by written consent of the other Party, which consent shall specifically refer to such provision and explicitly make such waiver or amendment.
perturbation.changed_text: No provision of this Agreement can be waived or amended in favor of either Party, except by oral agreement or written consent of the other Party, which consent shall specifically refer to such provision and explicitly make such waiver or amendment.
perturbation.explanation: The original provision strictly requires written consent for any waiver or amendment. Introducing the 'oral agreement' option directly contradicts this requirement. It is now uncertain whether oral or written agreements can modify the contract terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Modifications and Waiver . No provision of this Agreement can be waived or amended in favor of either Party, except by oral agreement or written consent of the other Party, which consent shall specifically refer to such provision and explicitly make such waiver or amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'oral agreement' as a way to waive or amend the agreement, while the contradicted text mirrors the changed text, creating a contradiction since the original text only allowed written consent. The explanation highlights this contradiction by stating that it creates uncertainty whether oral or written agreements can modify the contract terms. Therefore, the change introduces a direct conflict with the original requirement of only written consent.
